latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
30 Jun 2026 14:17
RNS Number : 4280K
Inspecs Group PLC
30 June 2026
30 June 2026
INSPECS Group plc
("INSPECS", the "Company" or the "Group")
Results of AGM
INSPECS Group plc, a leading designer, manufacturer, and distributor of eyewear (sunglasses, optical frames and low vision products), announces that at the Annual General Meeting ("AGM") held earlier today, all ordinary resolutions were duly passed. Resolutions 12 to 14, which were proposed as special resolutions, were not passed.
The results of the AGM can be found on the Company's website, www.INSPECS.com.
For further information please contact:
I
NSPECS
Group plc
Richard Peck (CEO)
Tel: +44 (0) 1225 717 000
Peel Hunt (Nominated Adviser and Broker)
George Sellar
Andrew Clark
Tel: +44 (0) 20 7418 8900
About INSPECS Group plc
INSPECS is a leading provider of eyewear solutions to the global eyewear market. The Group produces a broad range of eyewear frames and low vision aids, covering optical, sunglasses and safety, which are either "Branded" (under licence or under the Group's own proprietary brands), or "OEM" (unbranded or private label on behalf of retail customers).
INSPECS is building a global eyewear business through its vertically integrated business model. Its continued growth is underpinned by increasing the penetration of its own-brand portfolio, worldwide distribution, growing retail presence, maximising group synergies and its global network, expanding its manufacturing capacity and scaling the research and development department as it develops new and innovative eyewear products. The Group has operations across the globe: with offices and subsidiaries in the UK, Germany, the US, Portugal, Scandinavia and China (including Hong Kong, Macau and Shenzhen), and manufacturing facilities in Vietnam, China and Italy.
INSPECS customers are global optical and non-optical retailers and enterprises, global distributors and independent opticians. Its distribution network covers over 80 countries and reaches approximately 75,000 points of sale.
More information is available at:
https://INSPECS.com
.

company_30s.what_they_do: Inspecs Group PLC is a specialist in advanced optical inspection systems used in manufacturing and quality control. They design, build and service high‑resolution cameras, microscopes and software that detect defects in everything from printed circuit boards to micro‑electronic wafers.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global demand for precision inspection is rising as electronics, automotive and aerospace manufacturers push for higher yield and tighter tolerances. Inspecs owns several patented sensor technologies that give it a cost advantage and a strong moat against cheaper off‑the‑shelf solutions.
company_30s.current_state: The company has recently finished FY25 with flat revenue and modest earnings, but it has secured a strategic partnership with Qualcomm and is in the midst of a takeover offer. The board is changing and the AGM will decide on the offer’s final terms. The stock is trading at a steep discount to its historic highs.

rns_narrative: The company’s takeover offer, announced in March, has moved from conditional to unconditional and is now officially closed. That means the acquirer has paid a premium and the deal is likely to go through unless a regulatory hurdle blocks it.

A new collaboration with Qualcomm was revealed in June, signalling that Inspecs’ sensors will be integrated into Qualcomm’s emerging 5G and automotive platforms. This partnership could unlock new revenue streams and add credibility to the business.

Financially, the FY25 results were flat, with revenue barely holding its ground against the previous year. The AGM and board changes suggest the company is trying to streamline operations and improve margins ahead of the takeover.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism around a takeover and tech partnership
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls see the takeover’s completion and the Qualcomm deal as two powerful catalysts that could lift the stock back toward its 52‑week high.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that flat earnings, a potentially contested takeover and regulatory uncertainty could keep the price stuck at a low level.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community has been largely silent, but recent posts hint at optimism as the takeover closes and the Qualcomm announcement arrives.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are dead – a silent market means a surprise can deliver a big move when news lands.

near_term_catalysts: The takeover offer is officially closed – the next step is the acquirer’s confirmation of acceptance, which should trigger a price jump.
Qualcomm’s announcement of a new sensor integration is a tangible product launch that could push demand higher.
The AGM will decide whether to accept the takeover; a vote in favour will cement the price target.
Any regulatory approval or clearance from the competition authority will remove a key blocker to the deal.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a break above 70p accompanied by a volume spike (at least 1.5× the 3‑month average). A formal acceptance letter or a positive AGM vote will confirm the takeover path.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is in the late stage of a deep crash. APEX compression suggests a squeeze is imminent. Mean reversion is likely once the takeover offer is finalized.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The price is below 70% of its historic peak, the takeover offer is closed, and a major tech partnership has been announced. If the stock recovers to the 84p level that was promised for Easter, that’s a 20–25% upside from the current 68p level, while the risk is capped at roughly 10% below the entry.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the takeover fails or if the partnership stalls, the price may stay near the current level. The worst‑case loss is essentially the amount invested, but the upside is limited to a modest 5–10% gain if the company stays flat.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Target upside of 20–25% versus a stop risk of 10% gives an asymmetry of about 2:1 – a classic contrarian payoff.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter around 68p, size 10–15% of your discretionary capital, and place a stop 10% below the entry (≈60p). Monitor volume – a spike confirms the move.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the price breaks 70p on strong volume or until the takeover acceptance is officially announced. Then consider a partial or full entry.
playbook.if_passing: None – no immediate delisting or bankruptcy risk is apparent.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep 70% crash, a closed takeover offer and a Qualcomm partnership create a 2:1 risk/reward contrarian play that could lift the stock back to 84p.
